# Yan Pan — Founder & Applied AI Engineer

> Yan Pan (Yvette) is Founder & Applied AI Engineer at Zen Trading in San Francisco, building an agentic quantamental research and trading platform. Current work combines multi-factor ML/RL strategies, the Zentos CLI (an end-to-end agentic pipeline for external quant contributors), and LLM agents scoped to orchestration — model routing, factor mining, and research assistance — rather than autonomous trading. The underlying thesis is that agentic systems work best as a rigor-preserving layer over deterministic models, not a replacement for them.

## Main pages

- [About](https://yanpan.dev/index.html): Current focus at Zen Trading, interests and activities (incl. agentic math-education incubation), and condensed experience summary.
- [Resume](https://yanpan.dev/resume.html): Interactive resume — experience at Zen Trading, FedML / TensorOpera AI, Alibaba Cloud PAI, Tencent CV Lab; education at Carnegie Mellon University and Queen Mary University of London / Beijing University of Posts and Telecommunications; accelerators and sponsorships (SCALE GMI, NVIDIA Inception, Microsoft Azure, Lambda Labs, Fireworks AI, DigitalOcean); with a chatbot for natural-language Q&A.
- [Portfolio](https://yanpan.dev/portfolio.html): Featured project — multi-factor ML/RL strategy research, Zentos CLI agentic pipeline, and LLM agents in the ML/DL strategy stack. Previous work at TensorOpera AI (MLOps / Federated Learning SDK), Alibaba Cloud PAI (ML Platform / Privacy Computing), and Tencent CV Lab (CV Defect Detection Engineering).

## Writing

- [Human Written](https://yanpan.dev/blog.html): Index of personal essays and technical posts.
- [How I Overcame My Fear of Understanding Math Formulas When Reading Papers](https://yanpan.dev/blog-overcome-fear-math-formulas.html): Personal essay on getting past math anxiety when reading research papers.
- [Finding That Star in All the Noise](https://yanpan.dev/blog-finding-star-in-noise.html): On signal versus noise in the current AI landscape.
- [Human Written Matters](https://yanpan.dev/blog-human-written-matters.html): Why human-authored writing still matters in the LLM era.
- [Between Jobs, Between Homes](https://yanpan.dev/blog-between-jobs-between-homes.html): Personal essay on transitions.
- [Autoresearch and Quant Research](https://yanpan.dev/blog-autoresearch-quant-research.html): On automated research workflows in quantitative finance.

## Reading

- [Recent Reading](https://yanpan.dev/reading.html): Currently reading list — books across finance, economics, history, and technology.

## Optional

- [Data Shop](https://yanpan.dev/shop.html): Premium datasets for research, analysis, and AI/ML applications.

## Key facts

- **Current role**: Founder & Applied AI Engineer at Zen Trading (Oct 2024 – Present), San Francisco
- **Focus areas**: agentic quantamental research and trading platforms, multi-factor ML/RL strategy research, LLM agent orchestration, point-in-time safety, dual-framework backtest validation (vectorbt and QuantConnect Lean), declarative strategy specifications, sandboxed execution
- **Previous experience**: Founding Product Manager at FedML / TensorOpera AI (2022–2024), AI Product Manager at Alibaba Cloud PAI (2021–2022), Software Engineer Intern at Tencent CV Lab (2021)
- **Education**: MS in Information Technology, Carnegie Mellon University (2019–2022); BEng in Intelligent Science and Technology, Queen Mary University of London / Beijing University of Posts and Telecommunications Joint Program (2015–2019)
- **Contact**: yvette@zentraders.xyz
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/Yvette-0508
- **LinkedIn**: https://www.linkedin.com/in/yvette-pan-488247173/
- **Based in**: San Francisco Bay Area, California
